# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for edge computing security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

// Input validation utilities
const ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS = new Set([
  "name",
  "email",
  "role",
  "status",
  "department",
  "created",
  "updated",
]);

const MAX_KEY_SEGMENT_LENGTH = 128;
const MAX_QUERY_RESULTS = 100;

// Validate and sanitize a key segment to prevent KV key injection
function sanitizeKeySegment(input: string): string {
  if (!input || typeof input !== "string") {
    throw new Error("Invalid key segment: must be a non-empty string");
  }

  // Remove any null bytes, control characters, and path traversal attempts
  const sanitized = input
    .replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "") // Remove control characters
    .replace(/\.\./g, "")             // Remove path traversal
    .replace(/[\/\\]/g, "")           // Remove path separators
    .trim();

  if (sanitized.length === 0) {
    throw new Error("Invalid key segment: empty after sanitization");
  }

  if (sanitized.length > MAX_KEY_SEGMENT_LENGTH) {
    throw new Error(
      `Invalid key segment: exceeds maximum length of ${MAX_KEY_SEGMENT_LENGTH}`
    );
  }

  // Only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots, and @ (for emails)
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9._@\-]+$/.test(sanitized)) {
    throw new Error(
      "Invalid key segment: contains disallowed characters. Only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores, and @ are allowed."
    );
  }

  return sanitized;
}

// Validate that a filter parameter is in the allowlist
function validateFilterParam(param: string): string {
  const lower = param.toLowerCase();
  if (!ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(lower)) {
    throw new Error(
      `Invalid filter parameter: '${param}'. Allowed: ${[...ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS].join(", ")}`
    );
  }
  return lower;
}

// Validate userId format
function validateUserId(userId: string): string {
  const sanitized = sanitizeKeySegment(userId);
  // User IDs should be alphanumeric with optional hyphens (UUID-like)
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9\-]+$/.test(sanitized)) {
    throw new Error("Invalid user ID format");
  }
  if (sanitized.length > 64) {
    throw new Error("User ID too long");
  }
  return sanitized;
}

// Build KV key safely from validated components
function buildKey(
  ...segments: string[]
): Deno.KvKey {
  return segments.map((s) => sanitizeKeySegment(s)) as unknown as Deno.KvKey;
}

// Rate limiting with KV
async function checkRateLimit(
  kv: Deno.Kv,
  clientIp: string
): Promise<boolean> {
  const sanitizedIp = clientIp.replace(/[^a-fA-F0-9.:]/g, "").slice(0, 45);
  const key = ["ratelimit", sanitizedIp];
  const now = Date.now();
  const windowMs = 60_000; // 1 minute window
  const maxRequests = 60;

  const entry = await kv.get<{ count: number; windowStart: number }>(key);

  if (!entry.value || now - entry.value.windowStart > windowMs) {
    await kv.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now }, { expireIn: windowMs });
    return true;
  }

  if (entry.value.count >= maxRequests) {
    return false;
  }

  await kv
    .atomic()
    .check(entry)
    .set(
      key,
      { count: entry.value.count + 1, windowStart: entry.value.windowStart },
      { expireIn: windowMs }
    )
    .commit();

  return true;
}

// Sanitize user data before storing (strip any unexpected fields)
function sanitizeUserData(data: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, string> {
  const allowedFields = ["name", "email", "role", "status", "department"];
  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};

  for (const field of allowedFields) {
    if (data[field] !== undefined && data[field] !== null) {
      const value = String(data[field]).slice(0, 500);
      // Basic XSS prevention for stored data
      sanitized[field] = value
        .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
        .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
        .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
        .replace(/'/g, "&#x27;");
    }
  }

  return sanitized;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

serve(
  async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
    const url = new URL(req.url);
    const path = url.pathname;

    // CORS headers
    const corsHeaders = {
      "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
      "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS",
      "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
      "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
      "Cache-Control": "no-store",
    };

    if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
      return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders });
    }

    // Rate limiting
    const clientIp =
      req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
      req.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") ||
      "unknown";

    const allowed = await checkRateLimit(kv, clientIp);
    if (!allowed) {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later." }),
        { status: 429, headers: corsHeaders }
      );
    }

    try {
      // Route: GET /api/users - List/search users
      if (path === "/api/users" && req.method === "GET") {
        const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
        const limitParam = url.searchParams.get("limit");
        const limit = limitParam
          ? Math.min(Math.max(1, parseInt(limitParam, 10) || 10), MAX_QUERY_RESULTS)
          : 10;

        // Extract and validate filter parameters
        for (const [key, value] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
          if (key === "limit" || key === "offset") continue;

          try {
            const validatedParam = validateFilterParam(key);
            const sanitizedValue = sanitizeKeySegment(value);
            filters[validatedParam] = sanitizedValue;
          } catch (e) {
            return new Response(
              JSON.stringify({
                error: `Invalid filter: ${(e as Error).message}`,
              }),
              { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
            );
          }
        }

        const users: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];

        // If specific filter params are provided, use indexed lookups
        if (Object.keys(filters).length > 0) {
          for (const [filterParam, filterValue] of Object.entries(filters)) {
            // Use the index key pattern: ['index', filterParam, filterValue, userId]
            const prefix: Deno.KvKey = ["index", filterParam, filterValue];
            const iter = kv.list({ prefix }, { limit });

            for await (const entry of iter) {
              const userId = entry.key[3] as string;
              const userEntry = await kv.get(["users", userId]);
              if (userEntry.value) {
                users.push({
                  id: userId,
                  ...(userEntry.value as Record<string, unknown>),
                });
              }
            }
          }
        } else {
          // List all users with prefix scan
          const iter = kv.list({ prefix: ["users"] }, { limit });
          for await (const entry of iter) {
            const userId = entry.key[1] as string;
            users.push({
              id: userId,
              ...(entry.value as Record<string, unknown>),
            });
          }
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ data: users, count: users.length }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Route: GET /api/users/:id - Get user by ID
      if (path.startsWith("/api/users/") && req.method === "GET") {
        const segments = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
        if (segments.length !== 3) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid path" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const userId = validateUserId(segments[2]);

        // Check for filter param in query string for sub-resource lookup
        const filterParam = url.searchParams.get("field");

        if (filterParam) {
          const validatedFilter = validateFilterParam(filterParam);
          const key: Deno.KvKey = ["users", userId, validatedFilter];
          const entry = await kv.get(key);

          if (!entry.value) {
            return new Response(
              JSON.stringify({ error: "Field not found" }),
              { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
            );
          }

          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({
              data: { id: userId, field: validatedFilter, value: entry.value },
            }),
            { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const entry = await kv.get(["users", userId]);
        if (!entry.value) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "User not found" }),
            { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ data: { id: userId, ...(entry.value as Record<string, unknown>) } }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Route: POST /api/users - Create user
      if (path === "/api/users" && req.method === "POST") {
        let body: Record<string, unknown>;
        try {
          const text = await req.text();
          if (text.length > 10_000) {
            return new Response(
              JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body too large" }),
              { status: 413, headers: corsHeaders }
            );
          }
          body = JSON.parse(text);
        } catch {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        if (!body || typeof body !== "object" || Array.isArray(body)) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body must be a JSON object" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        // Generate a safe user ID
        const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
        const sanitizedData = sanitizeUserData(body);

        if (Object.keys(sanitizedData).length === 0) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({
              error:
                "No valid fields provided. Allowed: name, email, role, status, department",
            }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const now = new Date().toISOString();
        const userData = {
          ...sanitizedData,
          created: now,
          updated: now,
        };

        // Use atomic operation to store user and indexes
        let atomic = kv.atomic();
        atomic = atomic.set(["users", userId], userData);

        // Create index entries for searchable fields
        for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(sanitizedData)) {
          if (ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(field)) {
            atomic = atomic.set(["index", field, value, userId], true);
            // Also store individual field for sub-resource access
            atomic = atomic.set(["users", userId, field], value);
          }
        }

        const result = await atomic.commit();
        if (!result.ok) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Failed to create user" }),
            { status: 500, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ data: { id: userId, ...userData } }),
          { status: 201, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Route: PUT /api/users/:id - Update user
      if (path.startsWith("/api/users/") && req.method === "PUT") {
        const segments = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
        if (segments.length !== 3) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid path" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const userId = validateUserId(segments[2]);

        // Check user exists
        const existing = await kv.get<Record<string, string>>(["users", userId]);
        if (!existing.value) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "User not found" }),
            { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        let body: Record<string, unknown>;
        try {
          const text = await req.text();
          if (text.length > 10_000) {
            return new Response(
              JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body too large" }),
              { status: 413, headers: corsHeaders }
            );
          }
          body = JSON.parse(text);
        } catch {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        if (!body || typeof body !== "object" || Array.isArray(body)) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Request body must be a JSON object" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const sanitizedData = sanitizeUserData(body);
        const now = new Date().toISOString();
        const updatedData = {
          ...existing.value,
          ...sanitizedData,
          updated: now,
        };

        // Atomic update: remove old indexes, set new ones
        let atomic = kv.atomic().check(existing);
        atomic = atomic.set(["users", userId], updatedData);

        // Remove old index entries and create new ones for changed fields
        for (const [field, newValue] of Object.entries(sanitizedData)) {
          if (ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(field)) {
            const oldValue = existing.value[field];
            if (oldValue && oldValue !== newValue) {
              atomic = atomic.delete(["index", field, oldValue, userId]);
            }
            atomic = atomic.set(["index", field, newValue, userId], true);
            atomic = atomic.set(["users", userId, field], newValue);
          }
        }

        const result = await atomic.commit();
        if (!result.ok) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({
              error: "Failed to update user (concurrent modification)",
            }),
            { status: 409, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ data: { id: userId, ...updatedData } }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Route: DELETE /api/users/:id - Delete user
      if (path.startsWith("/api/users/") && req.method === "DELETE") {
        const segments = path.split("/").filter(Boolean);
        if (segments.length !== 3) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid path" }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const userId = validateUserId(segments[2]);

        const existing = await kv.get<Record<string, string>>(["users", userId]);
        if (!existing.value) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({ error: "User not found" }),
            { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        // Atomic delete: remove user and all indexes
        let atomic = kv.atomic().check(existing);
        atomic = atomic.delete(["users", userId]);

        // Remove index entries
        for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(existing.value)) {
          if (ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(field) && value) {
            atomic = atomic.delete(["index", field, value, userId]);
            atomic = atomic.delete(["users", userId, field]);
          }
        }

        const result = await atomic.commit();
        if (!result.ok) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({
              error: "Failed to delete user (concurrent modification)",
            }),
            { status: 409, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ message: "User deleted successfully" }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Route: GET /api/search - Advanced search with multiple criteria
      if (path === "/api/search" && req.method === "GET") {
        const query = url.searchParams.get("q");
        const field = url.searchParams.get("field");
        const limitParam = url.searchParams.get("limit");
        const limit = limitParam
          ? Math.min(Math.max(1, parseInt(limitParam, 10) || 10), MAX_QUERY_RESULTS)
          : 10;

        if (!query || !field) {
          return new Response(
            JSON.stringify({
              error: "Both 'q' (query) and 'field' parameters are required",
            }),
            { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
          );
        }

        const validatedField = validateFilterParam(field);
        const sanitizedQuery = sanitizeKeySegment(query);

        const results: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];

        // Use prefix-based search on the index
        const prefix: Deno.KvKey = ["index", validatedField, sanitizedQuery];
        const iter = kv.list({ prefix }, { limit });

        for await (const entry of iter) {
          const userId = entry.key[3] as string;
          const userEntry = await kv.get(["users", userId]);
          if (userEntry.value) {
            results.push({
              id: userId,
              ...(userEntry.value as Record<string, unknown>),
            });
          }
        }

        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ data: results, count: results.length, query: sanitizedQuery, field: validatedField }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      // Health check
      if (path === "/health" && req.method === "GET") {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ status: "ok", timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }),
          { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }),
        { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
      );
    } catch (error) {
      // Don't leak internal error details to clients
      console.error("Internal error:", error);
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Internal server error" }),
        { status: 500, headers: corsHeaders }
      );
    }
  },
  { port: 8000 }
);
